environment_types_note: Aligned with config/contracts/deployment_modes.json DEPLOY_MODE values. Legacy terms in older documents map as: public -> extranet, local_dev -> standalone.
direction_invariant: Machine sync direction is one-way extranet -> intranet and this is a hard invariant (mirrors deployment_modes.json invariants). Intranet user feedback (comments/reactions/ratings/adoption/requirements/tasks/notifications) never flows back to extranet through any channel.

identity_fields.instance_id: Unique ID for each deployment instance.
identity_fields.global_id: Stable UUID/ULID for objects that may sync across instances.
identity_fields.origin_instance_id: Instance that first created the object.
identity_fields.revision: Monotonic revision for mutable synced objects.
identity_fields.content_hash: Hash for immutable or append-only payloads.
identity_fields.visibility_scope: public/internal/restricted boundary used before any sync.

single_database.description: Public and intranet app instances point to one PostgreSQL database through a private network or VPN.
single_database.best_for: One trusted network or early deployment where operational simplicity matters.
risks: Public app must never expose database port
Intranet data boundary may be harder to reason about

database_replication.description: Use PostgreSQL physical or logical replication between databases.
database_replication.best_for: Read-only replicas or infrastructure teams comfortable operating PostgreSQL replication.
risks: Conflict handling is hard for two writable databases
Schema migration order must be strict
Sensitive intranet data may replicate unintentionally

app_level_outbox_inbox.description: Recommended for extranet plus intranet. Each app writes its local DB; intranet pulls selected public objects from an extranet GET feed, while signed export/import packages remain the manual fallback channel.
app_level_outbox_inbox.best_for: Clear security boundaries, one-way extranet-to-intranet sync, auditability, and replayable pull-only intranet deployments.

extranet_to_intranet_feed: generated_news ready/fallback review state and source lineage

intranet_to_public.rule: No intranet -> extranet machine channel exists or is planned in the feed. If this channel is ever built it is a manually exported, admin-approved package limited to the candidate items below; it must never carry user feedback objects (see direction_invariant) and must never enter GET /api/sync/feed.
candidate_items: non-sensitive data_source config changes if explicitly approved
taxonomy/domain pack updates if managed outside Git
aggregate source quality scores without user details if explicitly approved

sync_safety_rules: Objects with visibility_scope = restricted must never sync automatically.
Objects with visibility_scope = internal must not sync to public by default.
Secrets, tokens, cookies and credentials must be referenced by credential_ref and never included in sync packages or feed payloads.
Secret-like keys are detected through the shared privacy rule: token, secret, password, cookie, authorization, api_key, .env, client_secret and session.
Extranet-to-intranet sync is allowed for public source/raw/news/generated/report data.

Intranet feedback objects never sync back to extranet under any condition (hard invariant). A hypothetical intranet-to-public config/aggregate channel is planned_manual_only_not_implemented: manual package, explicit admin approval, never the feed.

Requirements, topic tasks, comments, reactions, ratings, activity events, notifications, notification preferences, object watchers and editorial decisions must not appear in extranet feed object_types.

feed_api.authentication: Authorization: Bearer token from SYNC_SERVICE_TOKENS; feed endpoints never use cookie session. Entries may be 'name:token' to name the consumer for audit/rotation, or a bare token (consumer recorded as token-<position>).
feed_api.access_audit: Every manifest/page read writes an audit log (actions sync_feed.manifest / sync_feed.read) recording the consumer identity, object_type, cursor range, record_count and has_more; cursors are opaque waterlines and contain no secrets.
feed_api.cursor_rule: Keyset cursor over updated_at/id with a strictly-greater-than filter; the publisher stays stateless and every page is replayable. next_cursor is derived from the last row scanned on the page, including rows filtered out by the secret/visibility redline, so records may be empty while next_cursor is non-null. null only means the request scanned no rows at all.

consumer_pull_rules.replay_lookback: To compensate for publisher-side long transactions committing rows with older updated_at than an already-advanced waterline, the consumer rewinds the persisted cursor by SYNC_PULL_REPLAY_LOOKBACK_SECONDS (300) on the first page of each object type per pull round. Duplicate events in the overlap window are absorbed by sync_inbox event_id idempotency (settled statuses applied/skipped/conflict). In-round pagination cursors never rewind, so a round strictly advances and cannot loop.
consumer_pull_rules.conflict_does_not_block_cursor: Pages that produce conflicts still advance the cursor and do not abort remaining object types. conflict is a settled inbox status: the feed event_id is a deterministic hash of object_type|global_id|revision, so the same object version is judged for conflict exactly once and resolution goes through the sync_conflicts APIs, not through re-pulling.
consumer_pull_rules.conflict_dedupe: Open conflicts are idempotent per (object_type, object_id): a repeated judgment refreshes the existing open row (seen_count/last_seen_at) instead of inserting a duplicate or re-sending notifications.
consumer_pull_rules.transport_failure: Manifest or page transport errors (unreachable extranet, invalid token, 4xx/5xx, invalid JSON) never raise out of the pull: they persist a SyncRun with status=failed (errors summary, per_object transport_failed) and set sync_cursors.last_status=failed for page-level failures, so GET /api/sync/health surfaces the failure immediately.

feed_api.scheduler_rule: When capability_sync_consumer and SYNC_PULL_ENABLED are true, scheduler enqueues sync_pull every SYNC_PULL_INTERVAL_SECONDS even when ingestion is disabled. If SYNC_FAILED_INBOX_AUTO_RETRY_ENABLED is true, the same scheduler loop also enqueues due failed-inbox retries through the local apply handler.

health_api.authentication: Cookie session plus super_admin. This endpoint is for local operators and never uses sync service tokens.
status_inputs: failed sync_inbox count and object_type breakdown
failed sync_inbox retry policy, due count, blocked-by-max-attempts count and next retry time
health_api.threshold_rule: warning if cursor age exceeds max(60, SYNC_PULL_INTERVAL_SECONDS) * 2; critical if cursor age exceeds max(60, SYNC_PULL_INTERVAL_SECONDS) * 6. Failed cursor status or error text is critical. Failed inbox records are critical because they require retry or operator inspection. Open conflicts are warning. A consumer with missing cursors is warning until the first successful pull creates waterlines.

retry_api.authentication: Cookie session plus super_admin. This is a local operator action and never uses sync service tokens.

query_params.object_type: Optional sync feed object type filter.
query_params.limit: Maximum failed inbox rows to retry, default 100, max 500.

retry_api.behavior: Select sync_inbox rows with status=failed, replay their stored record_json through the same apply handlers, update status/error_message/attempt_count/last_attempt_at, and write a SyncRun with direction=inbox_retry.

auto_retry.default: Same as sync_pull_effective on sync consumer deployments.
auto_retry.selection_rule: Only sync_inbox rows with status=failed, attempt_count < max attempts, and last_attempt_at + min(max_delay, base_delay * 2^(attempt_count-1)) <= now are selected. Manual retry-failed keeps the operator override and can retry failed rows immediately.
auto_retry.behavior: Auto retry creates a SyncRun with direction=inbox_auto_retry and reuses the same apply handler and counts_json shape as manual retry. It does not use sync service tokens and does not bypass secret/restricted/conflict checks.

first_phase_apply_handlers.idempotency: sync_inbox.event_id is the feed/package-level idempotency key; supported business objects upsert by object_global_id/global_id. Existing inbox rows with settled status (applied/skipped/conflict) are skipped, while failed rows keep record_json and may be replayed through POST /api/sync/inbox/retry-failed after dependency order is fixed.
first_phase_apply_handlers.conflict_rule: If local revision is newer than incoming, or the same revision has a different content_hash, write sync_conflicts and do not overwrite the local object. conflict settles the inbox event (deterministic event_id means one judgment per object version); the pull keeps advancing the cursor, and repeated judgments refresh the existing open conflict row instead of duplicating it.
first_phase_apply_handlers.conflict_resolution_v1: GET /api/sync/conflicts lists open/resolved/use_incoming/manual_merge/ignored/retry_after_dependency conflicts. POST /api/sync/conflicts/{id}/resolve records keep_local, ignored, or retry_after_dependency decisions with resolved_by_user_id/resolved_at and audit log. use_incoming applies the incoming payload through the same object apply handler. manual_merge is enabled for data_sources, daily_reports, and weekly_reports, requires merged_json, writes max(local_revision,incoming_revision)+1, and still uses the same object apply handler.

resolution_strategies.keep_local: Close the conflict as resolved without changing the business object.
resolution_strategies.ignored: Mark the conflict ignored with a reason.
resolution_strategies.retry_after_dependency: Mark the conflict for dependency retry.
resolution_strategies.use_incoming: Apply incoming_value_json through object handlers, bypassing only the original revision/hash conflict check.
resolution_strategies.manual_merge: For data_sources/daily_reports/weekly_reports, merge incoming_value_json with merged_json and apply a new revision through object handlers.

first_phase_apply_handlers.safety_rule: Payloads with restricted visibility or secret-like fields such as token/secret/password/cookie/authorization/api_key/.env/client_secret/session are not applied. Feed and manual sync package export must exclude such payloads instead of serializing them.
first_phase_apply_handlers.unsupported_object_types: Unsupported object types fail the record and remain auditable in sync_runs.counts_json.errors instead of being silently applied.
